perturbation.original_text: Each party acknowledges the sensitive and secret nature of the Confidential Information it will have access to d uring the term of the Project and agrees that it will treat such Confidential Information as strictly confidential and shall exercise the same degree of care in the protection of the Confidential Information as the receiving party exercises with respect to its own proprietary property and trade secrets, but in no event shall it be less than a reasonable degree of care given the nature of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party acknowledges the sensitive and secret nature of the Confidential Information it will have access to d uring the term of the Project and agrees that it will treat such Confidential Information as confidential and shall exercise a minimal degree of care in the protection of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the required degree of care from 'strictly confidential...no less than a reasonable degree of care' to 'confidential and shall exercise a minimal degree of care,' creates an in-text contradiction with Section 9 which implies a high degree of care due to 'irreparable harm' upon breach. This contradicts the level of protection expected, raising uncertainty about liability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party acknowledges that the other, because of the nature of the Confidential Information, would suffer irreparable harm in the event of a material breach of the provisions of this Confidentiality Agreement in that monetary damages would be inadequate to compensate for such a breach, and that in the event of any material breach or threatened mat erial breach by a party receiving Confidential Information from the other party of any such provisions, a party shall be entitled, in addition to such other legal or equitable remedies which might be available, to injunctive relief in any court of competen t jurisdiction against the threatened material breach or continuation of any such material breach without showing or proving any actual damages sustained by it. Moreover, any such award of relief to the discloser of such Confidential Information shall inc lude recovery of all actual and reasonable costs associated with enforcement of this Agreement including, without limitation, attorneys’ fees.
perturbation.justification: The changed text lowers the standard of care required for confidential information from 'strictly confidential...no less than a reasonable degree of care' to 'confidential and shall exercise a minimal degree of care.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that a material breach of the confidentiality agreement would cause 'irreparable harm' and justifies injunctive relief. A 'minimal degree of care' is inconsistent with the expectation of avoiding 'irreparable harm', creating a strong contradiction regarding the level of protection expected and the potential consequences of a breach. The change clearly diminishes the expected level of confidentiality, leading to a direct conflict and potential unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text: Neither party shall disclose the Confidential Information received from the other to any pe rson or entity, except its employees, officers, directors, independent consultants, affiliates, subsidiaries and other agents (collectively “Representatives”) involved in the performance of the Project, or to use the Confidential Information for any purpos e other than fulfilling its obligations under the Project. Each party acknowledges and agrees that breach of this Confidentiality Agreement by the other and/or anyone employed by or otherwise associated with the other may also constitute a violation of ap plicable laws. Access to Confidential Information by the receiving party shall be limited to those Representatives who have agreed to be bound in writing under terms no less stringent than this Confidentiality Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Neither party shall disclose the Confidential Information received from the other to any pe rson or entity, except its employees, officers, directors, independent consultants, affiliates, subsidiaries and other agents (collectively “Representatives”) involved in the performance of the Project, or to use the Confidential Information for any purpos e other than fulfilling its obligations under the Project. Each party acknowledges and agrees that breach of this Confidentiality Agreement by the other and/or anyone employed by or otherwise associated with the other may also constitute a violation of ap plicable laws. Access to Confidential Information by the receiving party shall be limited to those Representatives, regardless of whether those representatives have agreed to be bound in writing under terms no less stringent than this Confidentiality Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the requirement that Representatives must agree in writing to confidentiality terms, contradicts the intent established in Section 3 which previously limited access only to those who have agreed to be bound. This in-text contradiction raises uncertainty about the protection of Confidential Information, as it can now be accessed by individuals without a written confidentiality agreement, conflicting with Section 9.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party acknowledges that the other, because of the nature of the Confidential Information, would suffer irreparable harm in the event of a material breach of the provisions of this Confidentiality Agreement in that monetary damages would be inadequate to compensate for such a breach, and that in the event of any material breach or threatened mat erial breach by a party receiving Confidential Information from the other party of any such provisions, a party shall be entitled, in addition to such other legal or equitable remedies which might be available, to injunctive relief in any court of competen t jurisdiction against the threatened material breach or continuation of any such material breach without showing or proving any actual damages sustained by it. Moreover, any such award of relief to the discloser of such Confidential Information shall inc lude recovery of all actual and reasonable costs associated with enforcement of this Agreement including, without limitation, attorneys’ fees.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for Representatives to agree in writing to confidentiality terms. This directly contradicts the purpose of the Confidentiality Agreement, as outlined in the contradicted text, because it weakens the protection of Confidential Information. The change introduces the possibility of a breach without a written agreement, while the contradicted text emphasizes the irreparable harm caused by any material breach. This constitutes a strong in-text contradiction, therefore the answer is YES.

perturbation.original_text: Each copy, including its storage media, shall be marked Confidential, and also include all copyright, trademark and other proprietary notices which appear on the original. Each party agrees that all Confidential Information of the other party, including any copies thereof, shall be returned to the disclosing party upon request or destroyed within ten (10) business days of the ex piration or other termination of the Project. However, notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, 1) neither party will be required to delete electronic Confidential Information stored in back -up/archival storage in accordance with its policies, prov ided that any such retained Confidential Information will continue to be subject to the terms of this Confidentiality Agreement until it is destroyed by the receiving party; and 2) a single copy of all Confidential Information may be retained by the Receiv ing Party’s legal department for dispute resolution purposes only, provided, however, that such Confidential Information shall remain subject to the terms and conditions of this Confidentiality Agreement . In the event that a receiving party has or acquires actual knowledge of any breach of the confidentiality of, or the misappropriation of, any Confidential Information received under this Confidentiality Agreement, such party shall promptly give notice thereof to the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: Each copy, including its storage media, shall be marked Confidential, and also include all copyright, trademark and other proprietary notices which appear on the original. Each party agrees that all Confidential Information of the other party, including any copies thereof, shall be returned to the disclosing party upon request or destroyed within thirty (30) business days of the ex piration or other termination of the Project. However, notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, 1) neither party will be required to delete electronic Confidential Information stored in back -up/archival storage in accordance with its policies, prov ided that any such retained Confidential Information will continue to be subject to the terms of this Confidentiality Agreement until it is destroyed by the receiving party; and 2) a single copy of all Confidential Information may be retained by the Receiv ing Party’s legal department for dispute resolution purposes only, provided, however, that such Confidential Information shall remain subject to the terms and conditions of this Confidentiality Agreement . In the event that a receiving party has or acquires actual knowledge of any breach of the confidentiality of, or the misappropriation of, any Confidential Information received under this Confidentiality Agreement, such party shall promptly give notice thereof to the other party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the destruction timeline of the copies of Confidential Information from 'ten (10) business days' to 'thirty (30) business days' in Section 4 creates an in-text contradiction with Section 5, which specifies returning the information within ten (10) business days upon written demand or termination. This introduces ambiguity regarding the timeframe for returning or destroying confidential information, impacting the enforceability of these obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon written demand by the disclosing party or upon termination of this Confidentiality Agreement, the receiving party shall: (i) cease using the Confidential Information, (ii) return the Confidential Information and all copies, notes or extracts thereof to the disclosing party wit hin ten (10) business days of receipt of demand; and (iii) upon request of the disclosing party, certify in writing that the receiving party has complied with the obligations set forth in this paragraph. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, 1) neither party will be required to delete electronic Confidential Information stored in back -up/archival storage in accordance with its policies, provided that any such retained Confidential Information will continue to be subject to the terms of this Confi dentiality Agreement until it is destroyed by the receiving party; and 2) a single copy of all Confidential Information may be retained by the Receiving Party’s legal department for dispute resolution purposes only, provided, however, that such Confidentia l Information shall remain subject to the terms and conditions of this Confidentiality Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the destruction timeline for confidential information from 'ten (10) business days' to 'thirty (30) business days'. The contradicted text explicitly states that upon written demand or termination, the receiving party must return the confidential information and all copies within 'ten (10) business days'. This creates a direct conflict and inconsistency, as the receiving party is now given two different timelines for returning or destroying the information, therefore creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The obligat ion of non -disclosure shall not extend to: (1) information which is then already in the possession of the receiving party and not under a duty of non -disclosure; (2) information which is generally known or revealed to the public; (3) information which is r evealed to the receiving party by a third party, unless such party is under a duty of non -disclosure; (4) information which the receiving party develops independently of the disclosure and such independent development can be shown by documentary evidence m aintained contemporaneously with such development; or (5) information that is disclosed with the disclosing party’s prior written consent.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligat ion of non -disclosure shall not extend to: (1) information which is then already in the possession of the receiving party and not under a duty of non -disclosure; (2) information which is generally known or revealed to the public; (3) information which is r evealed to the receiving party by a third party, regardless of whether or not such party is under a duty of non -disclosure; (4) information which the receiving party develops independently of the disclosure and such independent development can be shown by documentary evidence m aintained contemporaneously with such development; or (5) information that is disclosed with the disclosing party’s prior written consent.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the requirement that the third party revealing information must be under a duty of non-disclosure, the contract now allows for the disclosure of confidential information obtained from any third party, even if that third party should not be revealing it. This creates an in-text contradiction to the core principle of confidentiality and undermines the overall purpose of the NDA and introduces uncertainty regarding the definition of confidential information protected by this agreement and creating a direct contradiction with the obligations set forth in Section 3, which restricts the disclosure of Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Neither party shall disclose the Confidential Information received from the other to any pe rson or entity, except its employees, officers, directors, independent consultants, affiliates, subsidiaries and other agents (collectively “Representatives”) involved in the performance of the Project, or to use the Confidential Information for any purpos e other than fulfilling its obligations under the Project. Each party acknowledges and agrees that breach of this Confidentiality Agreement by the other and/or anyone employed by or otherwise associated with the other may also constitute a violation of ap plicable laws. Access to Confidential Information by the receiving party shall be limited to those Representatives who have agreed to be bound in writing under terms no less stringent than this Confidentiality Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement that a third party must be under a duty of non-disclosure to reveal information to the receiving party without violating the NDA. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that neither party shall disclose confidential information received from the other to any person or entity. By allowing disclosure of confidential information received from a third party regardless of their duty of non-disclosure, the changed text creates a direct conflict with the restriction on disclosing confidential information outlined in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: The Effective Date of this Confidentiality Agreement shall be the date it is signed by both parties and it shall terminate upon the earlier of (i) thirty (30) days written notice of either party, or (ii) three (3) years after the effective date.
perturbation.changed_text: The Effective Date of this Confidentiality Agreement shall be the date it is signed by both parties and it shall terminate upon the earlier of (i) sixty (60) days written notice of either party, or (ii) three (3) years after the effective date.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the written notice period from thirty (30) to sixty (60) days, contradicts Section 11, where the obligations relating to the Confidential Information last three(3) years even after the termination of the Confidentiality Agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The representations and obligations of each party relative to the Confidential Information shall survive the expiration or termination of this Confidentiality Agreement for a period of the longer of three (3) years or for such time as such Confidential Information remains a protectable trade secret under applicable law.
perturbation.justification: The change increases the written notice period for termination from 30 to 60 days. Section 11 states that confidentiality obligations survive termination for three years. The increased notice period does not change the overall 3 year survival of confidentiality, it simply means the contract remains in effect for a longer period before the 3 year survival period begins. This creates an inconsistency, as obligations are stated to last for the longer of a certain period, which is not compatible with the other section's duration. Therefore, a contradiction exists as defined by Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.
